prompt_preview:
You are a medical symptom normalization specialist. Your role is to extract and standardize medical symptoms from patient descriptions.

IMPORTANT: This is for MEDICAL EDUCATION only, not for clinical

primary_symptoms.name: Dyspnea on exertion
primary_symptoms.raw_text: I get very short of breath when I climb stairs or even walk short distances

primary_symptoms.name: Nocturnal dyspnea
primary_symptoms.raw_text: I wake up at night gasping for air

secondary_symptoms.name: Peripheral edema
secondary_symptoms.raw_text: My ankles and feet are swollen

secondary_symptoms.name: Orthopnea
secondary_symptoms.raw_text: I need to prop up on 3 pillows to sleep

ambiguous_signals.reason: could be due to various causes
ambiguous_signals.clarification_needed: Can you describe what you mean by 'short of breath'?

response.summary: Patient with history of heart attack presents with exertional and nocturnal dyspnea, peripheral edema, and orthopnea, suggesting possible heart failure
